page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-192GB is a KVM-hypervised virtual server configured with 32 shared vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory. The underlying hardware features an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. This configuration provides 6.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU resources.

Benchmark results indicate average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core rating in the middle 50% and a strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression result in the top 25%. Single-core performance is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core performance landing in the bottom 10% and single-threaded bzip3 decompression in the bottom 25%. However, the server demonstrates strong capabilities in specialized tasks, scoring in the top 25% for PassMark CPU extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests. Memory bandwidth and database operations remain in the average tier.

Given the shared CPU allocation, this server offers a high-density memory profile at a qualitative cost advantage compared to dedicated-core instances. It is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads that can tolerate shared CPU scheduling, such as large-scale caching, multi-threaded data processing, and moderate database operations. It is less optimal for single-threaded, latency-sensitive applications due to its weak single-core performance.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-192GB is an x86_64 virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM. This server does not include local storage or GPU resources. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, alongside strong results in multi-threaded decompression, encryption, and physics simulations. However, single-core performance is weak, placing in the bottom 10% for stress-ng single-core tests. The system provides 6.0 GB of memory per core and supports AVX, AES, and SHA-NI instruction sets. Qualitatively, the shared CPU allocation offers a cost-effective option for memory-heavy, multi-threaded workloads such as caching, parallelized batch processing, and database operations that do not require dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-192GB is a KVM virtual server configured with 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 192 GB RAM. It lacks local storage and exhibits strong multi-threaded performance.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-192GB is a KVM virtual server featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A high-density virtual server featuring shared processors and substantial memory capacity optimized for parallelized, memory-intensive workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 32 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing 6.0 GB per core
Strong multi-threaded decompression and encryption performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for memory-intensive, shared-CPU workloads
